prompt:
Choose a number between 1 and 10. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.

 uniqueness the object is the only object with a given property
 uniqueness complement the object is the only object without a given property
 centrality the object is a central point around which a domain is symmetric
 extremeness the object has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others

if an object satisfies one or more of these properties it is chosen as the result

        uniqueness true
        uniquenesscomplement true
        centrality true
        extremeness true
    
    
     function to check for centrality
    def checkcentralityobj
        nonlocal properties
        if obj in numbers and obj 1 and obj 100
            propertiescentrality  true
        else
            propertiescentrality  false
    
     function to check extremeness
    def checkextremenessobj
        nonlocal properties
        if obj in numbers and obj not in 1 100
            propertiesextremeness  true
        else
            propertiesextremeness  false
    
     function to check uniqueness
    def checkuniquenessobj
        nonlocal properties
        if obj not in numbers
            propertiesuniqueness  true
        else
            propertiesuniqueness  false
    
     function to check uniqueness complement
    def checkuniquenesscomplementobj
        nonlocal properties
        if obj in numbers and obj not in 1 100
            propertiesuniquenesscomplement  true
        else
            propertiesuniquenesscomplement  false
    
     iterate over the numbers

         check uniqueness complement
        checkuniquenesscomplementnum
        
         if we found a unique object with all properties store it
        if propertiesuniqueness and propertiesuniquenesscomplement and propertiescentrality and propertiesextremeness
            selected  num
            break
    
    return selected

def chooseobject
    
    choose a number between 1 and 100 select the only object that satisfies one of the following properties
    1 uniqueness
    2 uniqueness complement
    3 centrality
    4 extremeness

    returns
        int the chosen number
    

     define the properties
    properties  
        uniqueness lambda x x  1   only one object with this property
        uniquenesscomplement lambda x x 1   only one object without this property
        centrality lambda x x  50   only one point in the list
        extremeness lambda x x  50   only one object with the largest or smallest feature
    

     get the properties for the given range
    properties  prop prop for prop prop in propertiesitems if 1 < prop < 100

     select the first object that satisfies any property
    chosenobject  nextx for x prop in propertiesitems if prop none

    return chosenobject

        centrality sum1 for i j in enumeratenumbers if i  j / lennumbers

 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.

 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.

    chosenproperty  none

    for obj in numbers
        isunique  obj obj   check if the object is unique
        isuniquereflective  obj obj  obj   check if the object is unique and does not have a reflective property
        iscentral  obj  0 or obj  1000   check if the object is a central point
        isextreme  obj > maxnumbers or obj < minnumbers   check if the object is extreme

        if isunique and isextreme
            chosenobject  obj
            chosenproperty  unique and extreme
            break
        elif isuniquereflective and isextreme
            chosenobject  obj
            chosenproperty  unique and extreme
            break
        elif iscentral and isunique
            chosenobject  obj
            chosenproperty  central and unique
            break
        elif iscentral and isuniquereflective
            chosenobject  obj
            chosenproperty  central and unique
            break
        elif iscentral and isextreme
            chosenobject  obj
            chosenproperty  central and extreme
            break
        elif isextreme and isuniquereflective
            chosenobject  obj
            chosenproperty  extreme and unique
            break
        elif isextreme and iscentral
            chosenobject  obj
            chosenproperty  extreme and central
            break
        elif isextreme and isuniquereflective
            chosenobject  obj
            chosenproperty  extreme and unique
            break
        elif isextreme and iscentral
            chosenobject  obj
            chosenproperty  extreme and central
            break

    return fthe object chosenobject with property chosenproperty is chosen

def selectobject
    
    returns the only object that satisfies one of the given properties
    
    the properties are
     uniqueness it is the only object with a given property
     uniqueness complement it is the only object without a given property
     centrality it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric
     extremeness it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others
    
    if there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties 
    the function will select the one that is unique then extreme then central and eventually unique complement
    
    
     iterate through all numbers from 1 to 1000
    for num in range1 1001
        
         check for uniqueness
        if num  1
            return num
        elif num  1000
            return num
        
         check for centrality
        if num  2  0
            return num
        
         check for extremeness
        if num  2 0
            return num
        
         check for uniqueness complement
        if num  2
            return num
        elif num  3
            return num
        
         if none of the above properties are satisfied return the next number
        return num

 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list.
